data.ev1_desc: Reservoir level reached 18%. Automatic pumping triggered.
data.ev2_desc: Consumption 34% above average. Alert sent to operations team.
data.ev3_desc: Flow sensor calibration PTP-01 to PTP-04 completed successfully.
data.ev4_desc: 147 IoT sensors activated. Water and energy monitoring started.
data.ev5_desc: Water and energy consumption report for May/2026 available for download.
data.ev6_desc: Sensor PTP-03 Silvanópolis reconnected after 8-minute signal loss.

dashboard.stat_model_intro: The diagnostics above are generated by two complementary stages. For each series, parameters are calculated independently using only the values of that series.
dashboard.stat_model_p1: A value x is classified as out of pattern if:
dashboard.stat_model_p2: Quartiles and Tukey's fence define the control limits:
dashboard.stat_model_p2b: Additional guard: the relative deviation from the median must exceed 30% to avoid false positives in very stable series:
dashboard.stat_model_p3: For each anomalous point, the z-score is calculated relative to the normal distribution of operating values (μ, σ):
dashboard.stat_model_p4: The two-tailed p-value is obtained via the normal cumulative distribution function Φ:
dashboard.stat_model_p5: A low p-value (e.g. p = 0.3%) indicates only a 0.3% probability of that value occurring under normal operating conditions — it does not identify the cause, it merely signals that the value is not expected.

monitoring.title: Real-Time Water Monitoring
monitoring.subtitle: Continuous supervision platform for water supply systems in the municipalities of Silvanópolis and Miranorte, in the State of Tocantins.
monitoring.smart_city_text: Integrated with the Smart City 4.0 concept, this system connects physical infrastructure to a digital intelligence layer — sensors, telemetry and real-time analysis — to make public management more efficient, transparent and responsive. All processes are continuously and permanently supervised by artificial intelligence algorithms that monitor operational patterns, automatically identify deviations and anomalies, and issue immediate alerts to designated managers or technicians — ensuring each occurrence is handled by the right person, at the right time, before it becomes a critical issue for the population.
monitoring.data_decision_text: Municipal managers and technical teams monitor reservoir levels, pump flow rates and daily production without relying on on-site inspections — reducing operational costs and response time to failures or pressure drops.
monitoring.incident_text: Anomalies in consumption patterns or pump failures are automatically detected. The team is alerted in real time and can act before a supply disruption affects the population — ensuring continuity of the essential treated water service.
monitoring.sustainability_text: Precise pump control reduces electricity consumption and water waste. With historical data and trend projections, management can plan preventive maintenance and investments based on concrete evidence.
